text: The electrochemical oxidation of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) into 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid (FDCA) has been proposed as a new sustainable route for the synthesis of bio-based polymers. However, the transformation is often hampered by the instability of HMF in alkaline conditions that are necessary for the functioning of the catalysts usually employed. Herein we report the alkaline degradation of HMF and observe a selective Cannizzaro disproportionation into 5-hydroxymethyl-2-furancarboxylic acid (HMFCA) and dihydroxymethylfuran (DHMF). In contrast with HMF, the Cannizzaro products are stable in strongly alkaline solution up to 80 °C. Both HMFCA and DHMF are then directly electrooxidized into FDCA with a high faradaic efficiency of ca. 98% at transition metal based working electrodes. Based on these results, an “indirect” electrochemical route that allows the transformation of HMF at a much higher concentration than the previously reported and with a higher substrate and base concentration, thus reducing the accumulation of humins and affording higher space–time yields at technical scale by enabling current densities above 1 A cm⁻².
